title: Minimalist Code Counter
summary: Brutalist loading screen or hero state with a single monospace counter [091] centered on pure black. Ultra-reduced interface language that conveys precision, experimental tech, and high-end restraint.
typography: Monospace, all-caps counter with square brackets [091]. Small letterspacing, tight kerning. Appears to be a fixed-width system font (likely Courier or similar).
layout: Dead-center composition. Counter floats in the void with breathing room. Grid/linear structure referenced in site content — suggests everything else is hidden or progressively revealed.
takeaways: Use a single data point (counter/index) as the sole entry point to signal experimental or research-driven brand positioning
Pair monospace with extreme whitespace and pure black for instant credibility in tech/creative fields
Hide primary navigation/content initially — make visitors wait or interact to unlock the full experience
Brackets and numerical indexing suggest catalog/archive language — reusable for portfolio or product studios
Pure black + white contrast eliminates distraction; forces focus on interaction and progression

title: Neon pixelated grid chaos
summary: A vibrant y2k-inspired feed interface layered with a high-contrast monochrome figure, creating visual tension between chaotic neon color blocking and stark geometric forms. The grid-based layout feels both playful and glitchy.
typography: Sans-serif UI labels appear minimal and utilitarian in small scale; focus is on visual elements over typographic hierarchy.
layout: Dense grid-based composition with small square modules (pixel blocks) arranged in irregular clusters. Heavy use of white space breaks between blocks creates visual rhythm. Figure positioned off-center with layered depth through contrast.
takeaways: Use neon color blocking in discrete geometric units to create visual interest without symmetry
Layer high-contrast monochrome figures over busy backgrounds to add focal points and graphic punch
Grid-based layouts feel modular—allow uneven clustering rather than rigid alignment for dynamic energy
Pair bright complementary colors (magenta + cyan, yellow + purple) for that y2k pop
Small UI affordances (icons, buttons) at bottom don't need to match the chaos—understated nav creates breathing room
